title: Workshop: Build a Neighborhood Library Shelf
summary: Workshop: Build a Neighborhood Library Shelf uses an original library shelf build scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html elements nesting, html attributes, html text headings lists, html links urls without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real library shelf build artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

steps.title: Create the metadata container · library shelf build checkpoint 1 · workshop
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Create the metadata container in the existing library shelf build artifact, then rerun every accumulated check. Work context: library shelf build.
steps.why: Workshop: Build a Neighborhood Library Shelf keeps earlier work active while adding one testable HTML increment. This is checkpoint 1 in the library shelf build.

content.title: Build increment 1
content.text: Edit the existing library shelf build artifact. Run the checks, inspect the preview, and preserve every earlier passing requirement.

hints: Workshop: Build a Neighborhood Library Shelf: first isolate the evidence required by “Create the metadata container · library shelf build checkpoint 1.”
Workshop: Build a Neighborhood Library Shelf: compare the current artifact with the named code goal for step 1.
Workshop: Build a Neighborhood Library Shelf: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “Create the metadata container · library shelf build checkpoint 1.”

options.text: Library shelf build case 3: heading rank is a font-size control, so smaller-looking sections should always use h6.
Library shelf build case 3: select text elements for the relationship they communicate, then use CSS later for visual scale.
Library shelf build case 3: every sentence should be a heading because headings receive stronger default styling.
